summary: Large ground.c decompilation/matching PR that raised melee/gr/ground.c to 83.11% over 23.53 KB. It added C implementations or decomp scratch markers for many Ground_801Cxxxx functions, introduced local ground user-data typing, improved stage archive/fog/callback struct definitions, corrected several function prototypes, and converted the corresponding GALE01 scratches entries to OK. There were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Replaced several NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies in ground.c with matched or partially matched C, including Ground_801C14D0, Ground_801C1E94, Ground_801C36F4, Ground_801C3FA4, Ground_801C4FAC, and Ground_801C5878.

Left several difficult functions as decomp scratch placeholders using the project's `/// #...` convention, including Ground_801C0C2C, Ground_801C20E0, Ground_801C28CC, Ground_801C2FE0, Ground_801C34AC, and Ground_801C466C.
Added a local `mapData` struct in ground.c with explicit offsets, bitfields, velocity/position fields, and padding to support ground GObj user data handling.

Implemented ground GObj creation/setup logic in Ground_801C14D0: creates a GObj, allocates user data, initializes map_id and flags, loads stage archive joints, applies stage scale, sets up material/GX links, and installs ground procs.

Implemented fog setup/update paths: Ground_801C1E94 loads fog descriptors from stage archive callbacks, attaches HSD_Fog to a GObj, scales fog start/end by stage param, and updates camera background color; Ground_801C4FAC adjusts fog start/end based on camera eye vectors and stage_info vectors.
Typed stage_info.x12C as `HSD_GObj*` and added `GET_FOG(gobj)` in baselib gobj.h, replacing an anonymous nested pointer layout used to reach fog color.
Expanded `StageCallbacks` flags into a union with named bitfields `flags_b0` through `flags_b7`, enabling direct matching of individual callback flag tests.
Expanded `UnkStageDat` typing by introducing `struct UnkStageDat_x8_t` with camera descriptor, fog descriptor, S16Vec3 array, joint-depth array, and size fields, plus a static assert for size 0x34.

Adjusted prototypes for better typing and matching, such as Ground_801C36F4 taking `int map_id` and `UNK_T joint`, Ground_801C3FA4 taking `int depth`, Ground_801C4FAC taking `HSD_CObj* cobj`, grDisplay_801C5F60 taking an `int code`, and gm_8016B498 returning bool.
Updated un_2FC9.h to include gr/forward.h and type un_8031C2EC as `int un_8031C2EC(StageInfo*)`, used by Ground_801C5878.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt for the ground address range by changing many prior percentage scratches to `OK:0x...`, preserving scratch IDs and parent metadata.
smart_moves: Promoted opaque stage and fog layouts into reusable header types rather than continuing to dereference anonymous nested placeholders in ground.c.
Used bitfields for `StageCallbacks.flags` and ground user-data flags, which matches the source-level access pattern visible in code such as `flags_b1`, `flags_b2`, and `x10_flags.bN`.
Added `GET_FOG` alongside existing HSD_GObj access macros, making fog object access consistent with `GET_COBJ`, `GET_JOBJ`, and `GET_LOBJ`.
Added a `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct UnkStageDat_x8_t) == 0x34)` after refining an archive substructure, reducing the risk of silent layout drift.
Kept forward declarations in gr/forward.h, including `StageInfo`, to support cross-header typing without over-including stage headers.
Used exact source-line asserts and OSReport strings in new C bodies, which often matter for matching codegen and data references in this project.
Introduced small local/inlined helpers such as a local `fabsf`, `foo`, and `alloc_user_data_ground`, suggesting code was shaped around compiler output rather than only readability.
decomp_lessons: When matching ground/stage code, first type the archive payloads and stage callback flags; many functions become simpler once `UnkStageDat_x8_t`, `StageCallbacks.flags_bN`, and fog/camera descriptor fields are named.
GObj object payloads should use typed access macros where available. Adding `GET_FOG` let ground.c treat stage_info.x12C as an HSD_GObj and avoid hard-coded nested offsets.
For stage code, preserve line-specific assert text and OSReport file/line literals; these strings and immediate values are part of the matching surface.
Bitfield ordering and width are decomp-sensitive. This PR used byte-sized flag bitfields for both callback flags and ground map user data rather than masking raw integers everywhere.
Scratch bookkeeping matters: once code is accepted, update config/GALE01/scratches.txt to `OK` for all relevant address entries so future agents do not chase already-accepted scratches.
A partial PR can still be valuable when it combines matched functions with explicit `/// #` scratch markers for remaining hard functions, keeping unmatched work searchable and isolated.
naming_conventions: Ground functions retain address-based names in the form `Ground_801Cxxxx`.
Unknown struct fields use offset-style names such as `x12C`, `x6E4`, `unk8`, `x2C`, and `x30` until semantics are known.
Bitfields are named by bit position as `flags_b0` through `flags_b7` or `x10_flags.b0` through `x10_flags.b7`.
Stage archive substructure was named `UnkStageDat_x8_t`, encoding its owning struct and field offset.
Ground user-data typedef was introduced as `mapData`, with known semantic fields such as `map_id`, `selfVelX/Y/Z`, and `posX/Y/Z` mixed with offset placeholders.
HSD_GObj payload macros follow `GET_*` naming, e.g. `GET_COBJ`, `GET_FOG`, `GET_JOBJ`, and `GET_LOBJ`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `/// #FunctionName` scratch markers for functions still not represented as full C in the diff.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in Ground_801C5878, an explicit stack-shaping tactic.
Added a local inline `fabsf` instead of relying only on library math, likely to match compiler inlining and floating-point code shape.
Used exact assert-line macro `assert_line(line, cond)` with `__assert("ground.c", line, #cond)` to match original assert metadata.
Used HSD_JObj traversal helpers (`HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjGetNext`, `HSD_JObjGetParent`) in Ground_801C3FA4/Ground_801C4100 instead of local ad hoc accessors after typing became available.
Used a comma-expression allocation assignment `temp_r3 = (0, HSD_MemAlloc(0x204));` in Ground_801C14D0, which is a recognizable codegen-shaping idiom in decomp work.
Preserved precise floating-point thresholds and calculations in fog code, including sqrtf distance calculations and minimum start/end clamps.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish the scratch-marker functions Ground_801C0C2C, Ground_801C20E0, Ground_801C28CC, Ground_801C2FE0, Ground_801C34AC, or Ground_801C466C.
Search for subsequent corrections to Ground_801C2090 or fog color getter/setter behavior because this PR changed fog access through GET_FOG.
Look for later moves of local helpers or TODOs from ground.c into shared headers, especially fabsf or JObj traversal helpers.
Search later stage archive PRs for further refinement of `UnkStageDat_x8_t` and `StageCallbacks` bitfield names.
